model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Lehigh holds a slight but consistent edge across predictive metrics, ranking 284th in KenPom and 282nd in Torvik compared to Prairie View A&M's 291st and 292nd respectively, with a superior adjusted efficiency margin (-10.38 vs -11.1). The Mountain Hawks' offense is meaningfully better (AdjO 102.8 vs 101.1), and while Prairie View A&M has a marginally better defense, Lehigh's offensive advantage is larger. Playing in the stronger Patriot League and carrying momentum from a convincing 74-60 conference tournament championship win over Boston University, Lehigh's balanced scoring attack led by Nasir Whitlock gives them the edge in what projects as a tight 16-seed play-in game.

reasoningStep.title: South 16 Play-In: Lehigh vs Prairie View A&M
reasoningStep.summary: Lehigh is favored slightly based on superior KenPom/Torvik rankings, better adjusted offense, stronger conference, and a more convincing conference tournament run. Prairie View A&M's slightly better defense and hot three-point shooting in the SWAC final keep this close, but Lehigh's overall profile is marginally stronger.
evidence: Lehigh AdjO 102.8 vs PVAMU 101.1; Lehigh AdjD 113.1 vs PVAMU 112.2
Lehigh 18-16 record as Patriot League champions (beat BU 74-60)
Prairie View A&M 17-17 record as SWAC champions (beat Southern 72-66)
Patriot League is generally considered a stronger conference than the SWAC
Both teams feature balanced scoring with multiple double-figure scorers in their championship games

result.text: Prairie View A&M Claims SWAC Tournament Title - Southwestern Athletic Conference Skip To Main Content Southwestern Athletic Conference Main Navigation Menu Now Loading: Men's Basketball Sport Navigation Menu Men's Basketball 3/14/2026 9:54:00 PM Prairie View A&M Claims SWAC Tournament Title Story Links Box Score ATLANTA, Georgia- The Prairie View A&M Panthers shot 50 percent from three-point range enroute to a 72-66 win against the Southern Jaguars at Gateway Center Arena on Saturday. With the win PVAMU claimed the 2026 SWAC Men's Basketball Tournament championship title and secured the Southwestern Athletic Conference's automatic bid to the NCAA Division I Men's Basketball Tournament. Prairie View A&M had four players score in double figures led by Lance Williams, who had 18 points and five assists. Cory Wells tacked on 16 points, six rebounds and three blocks, while Dontae Horne added 15 points, five assists and four steals. Corey Dunning pulled down five offensive rebounds to lead the Prairie View A&M offense, which grabbed 10 boards on the offensive glass and turned them into 10 second-chance points. Michael Jacobs paced the Jaguars offensive attack tallying a team-high 19 points on 7-of-17 shooting from the field. SU’s AJ Barnes contributed18 points on 6-of-12 shooting for the floor and Cam Amboree scored ten points to round out the list of Southern double-digit contributors. After jumping out to a 24-17 advantage, Prairie View A&M went on a 10-0 run with 7:23 left in the first half, culminating in a three from Wells to increase its lead to 34-17. Southern would later trim some of the Panthers’ lead, but PVAMU entered halftime with a double-digit advantage at 41-28. Prairie View A&M relied on its three-point shooting in the period, knocking down six shots to account for 18 of its 41 points. Prairie View A&M started tacking on points in the second half almost immediately, going on a 6-0 run finished off by Williams' three to increase the Panthers’ lead to 47-28 with 18:08 to go in the contest. The Jaguars narrowed the margin late, but the Panthers held on down the stretch for the 72-66 championship victory. PVAMU’s Horne was named Tournament Most Valuable Player, highlighting standout performances throughout the duration of tournament. He was also named to the SWAC All-Tournament Team, joined by fellow teammate Williams, who earned All-Tournament Team honors following his impressive performance in the title game. The championship marks a major milestone for the Panthers as they now turn their focus to the national stage in the upcoming NCAA Men’s Basketball Tournament. Now I have a comprehensive picture. Let me compile my analysis.

**Summary of findings:**

**Lehigh (18-16, Patriot League champions)**
- KenPom: #284, AdjEM -10.38, AdjO 102.8, AdjD 113.1
- Torvik: #282, AdjOE 102.5, AdjDE 112.7
- Won Patriot League tournament as #2 seed, beating BU 74-60
- Led by Nasir Whitlock (18 pts in championship), balanced scoring with 4 players in double figures
- 19th-year head coach Brett Reed

**Prairie View A&M (17-17, SWAC champions)**
- KenPom: #291, AdjEM -11.1, AdjO 101.1, AdjD 112.2
- Torvik: #292, AdjOE 101.0, AdjDE 111.9
- Won SWAC tournament, beating Southern 72-66 shooting 50% from three
- Led by Lance Williams (18 pts), Cory Wells (16 pts), Dontae Horne (15 pts)
- 10th-year head coach Byron Smith

Lehigh has a slight edge in KenPom (#284 vs #291) and Torvik (#282 vs #292), with a better adjusted offense. Prairie View has a marginally better defense. Lehigh has a better record (18-16 vs 17-17) and the Patriot League is generally considered a stronger conference than the SWAC. This is very close but Lehigh has the edge across most metrics.
